description.p: Kductor is a KDE-native desktop application for orchestrating multiple Claude Code AI agents working in parallel across isolated git worktrees.

ul.li: Spawn multiple Claude Code agents per workspace
Real-time streaming output with markdown rendering
Built-in diff viewer with All/Committed/Pending modes
Push, create PRs, and merge — GitHub and Gitea supported
System tray with live agent status and notifications
Persistent sessions and conversation history across restarts

description.p: A major hardening and usability release.

ul.li: Security: fixed shell command injection via branch names; agent permission mode is now an explicit, visible setting; transcripts are stored owner-only; untrusted agent output can no longer trigger remote resource fetches.

Correctness: agents resume their own session by id; tool results and agent git events are detected again; conflicted merges are reported as failures; diffs show staged changes and new-file content; persistence is atomic.
UX: multi-line prompt with history, off-thread diffs with loading states, operation progress, commit messages, cost display, and a workspace actions menu.
Accessibility: keyboard navigation, accessible names, theme-correct colors, and reduced-motion support throughout.

description.p: Default model setting is now honored when starting agents, new model options, random city names prefilled for new workspaces, main window restore from tray, and an automated test suite with CI.
Initial release with full agent orchestration, diff viewer, PR workflow, and KDE integration.
